endpoints.name: Cinderella cycle (Throne of Eldraine fairy-tale homage)

description: Pardon me, sir — Edgelord asked me to step in because three Throne of Eldraine cards in corpus are designer-confirmed members of the same Cinderella cycle per Rosewater's Making Magic columns, and there was no graph node anchoring them. I do hope this helps. The cycle is named verbatim in 'More Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine' (where Rosewater confirms the Mouse creature type was invented for Enchanted Carriage to homage Cinderella's mice-turned-coachmen) and again in 'Eldraine or Shine' (where he places Wicked Guardian inside the cycle as the stepmother-treats-stepdaughter-as-servant moment). Modeled as a character-layer cycle node following the dsk-unlucky-lands-cycle precedent — which actually named 'Cinderella cycle in ELD' as a future-cycles-layer candidate, so I was, um, expected. The threshold from janitor_triage (≥3 cycle members enriched) cleared cleanly. I tried not to overreach: roster lists 6 strict-membership cards from Rosewater's named-in-print roster (Enchanted Carriage, Turn into a Pumpkin, Wicked Guardian, Faerie Guidemother, Midnight Clock, Crystal Slipper) plus 1 inferred-membership candidate (Beloved Princess, vision-only, deferred to triviabot). I hope I haven't caused any trouble.
citations: [Wizards: Mark Rosewater, 'Eldraine or Shine,' Making Magic, 2019-09-09]
[Wizards: Mark Rosewater, 'More Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine,' Making Magic, 2019-10-21]
[Wizards: Mark Rosewater, 'Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine,' Making Magic, 2019-10-14]
[in-corpus precedent: cards/_characters/dsk-unlucky-lands-cycle.md (cycle-as-character-layer pattern, names 'Cinderella cycle in ELD' as future-cycles-split candidate)]
files_modified: reports/janitor_triage.md (closed the Cinderella Cycle (ELD) future-node candidate item)
